mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals improving household demand and rising expected corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the trend is measured using real-time RSAFS releases.
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2020-10-15 the latest available RSAFS observation was August 2020 at 537526 million dollars
failure_condition: Reject if walk-forward tests show 20-day S&P 500 returns following positive 3-month RSAFS momentum are not higher than returns following negative momentum, or if the Rank IC is below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating household income, weaker consumption, and higher recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days after the labor deterioration becomes visible in real-time UNRATE data.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 monthly observations
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2020-10-15 the latest available UNRATE observation was September 2020 at 7.9 percent
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases and their mean forward return is not below the unconditional mean at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near the effective lower bound and CPI inflation is not accelerating, monetary conditions keep discount rates compressed without forcing tightening expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.50 percent and 3-month CPIAUCSL inflation is less than or equal to its trailing 12-month pace
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2020-10-15 the latest available FEDFUNDS observation was September 2020 at 0.09 percent
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate and non-accelerating-inflation trigger does not deliver higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than other periods, or if the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL has the wrong sign in more than half of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rebound in industrial production signals normalization of output, inventories, and operating leverage after a shock, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations recover.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases for at least 2 of the prior 3 monthly observations after a 6-month decline
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2020-10-15 the latest available INDPRO observation was August 2020 at 101.3605 in the real-time snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered post-decline INDPRO rebounds are followed by below-median 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the estimated return response is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A strong upturn in housing starts signals future construction activity, durable-goods demand, and credit-sensitive household confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 6-month percent change crosses above positive 10 percent
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2020-10-15 the latest available HOUST observation was August 2020 at 1416 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns after the HOUST crossing are not above the unconditional 60-day return in out-of-sample walk-forward tests, or if hit rate falls below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI inflation while the policy rate is not rising signals a negative real-rate squeeze and future tightening risk, reducing equity valuation support and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month annualized CPIAUCSL inflation crosses above 4 percent while FEDFUNDS has not increased over the prior 3 monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if CPI acceleration conditional on flat FEDFUNDS does not have a negative coefficient in walk-forward predictive regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A renewed contraction in payroll employment signals falling labor income and a relapse in economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors revise near-term earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change crosses below zero after at least 2 consecutive positive monthly changes
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2020-10-15 the latest available PAYEMS observation was September 2020 at 141720 thousand persons
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS contraction triggers do not produce lower 5-day S&P 500 returns than non-trigger days, or if the average triggered return is positive and statistically significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in real GDP growth combined with falling unemployment signals broad cyclical repair in output and labor income, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through higher expected earnings.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth crosses above zero and UNRATE declines over the latest 3 monthly observations
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2020-10-15 the latest available GDPC1 observation in the snapshot was 2020 Q2
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1 recovery and UNRATE decline trigger fails to outperform the unconditional 60-day S&P 500 return, or if either variable alone fully explains the signal and the interaction adds no out-of-sample predictive value.
